rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kelsey-Hayes Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kelsey-Hayes Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIRA B. JOHNSON, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PERSONAL et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs¿ Complaint For Wrongful Death And Survival
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs¿ complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to state facts sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages.
